to_buildworld.pivot_target: Open-source developer tools solving Big Tech technical gaps

build_now.description: Git-native prompt regression testing with judge calibration and trace-linked eval scoring for any LLM CI/CD pipeline
build_now.biblical_pattern: PAT-034 — Nehemiah 4:13-14 (guards at gaps in the wall)
build_now.key_differentiator: Judge calibration — validates that your LLM judge agrees with humans on your specific task before trusting it as a CI gate.

ship_now.description: Git-native PR semantic intent verifier. Verifies that PR code changes fulfill the stated intent in the PR description.
ship_now.biblical_pattern: PAT-036 — Romans 7:7 (The law makes violations visible)
ship_now.key_differentiator: The only open-source tool that verifies whether a PR's code changes fulfill its stated intent — pre-merge, with a CI gate, for any git repo.
ship_now.status: PROTOTYPE COMPLETE — SHIP TO PYPI NOW

build_next.description: Behavioral contract enforcement for LLM function calls — @contract decorator + versioned behavioral specs + CI gate + drift detection
build_next.biblical_pattern: PAT-035 — Acts 2:1-13 (Pentecost — many languages, one coherent message contract)
build_next.key_differentiator: The only library that defines, versions, and enforces behavioral (not just structural) contracts on LLM function calls, provider-agnostic, with drift detection.
build_next.status: DESIGN COMPLETE — BUILD AFTER prompt-lock v0.1

notes: Four confirmed green-field tools in pipeline. drift-guard BUILT — not just designed. 450+ lines of real Python code. Romans 7:7 is the most precise description of the intent-execution gap in all of literature. 'I would not have known what sin was had it not been for the law.' drift-guard makes the PR description the law. The violations become visible. The year of AI quality begins.
